understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to navigate a grid-based puzzle, activating and stepping onto goal cells by matching your piece's shape, color, and rotation to each goal's requirements. Levels contained multiple goals, and completing all goals in a level advanced you to the next. Modifier tiles on the grid changed your piece's attributes to help meet these requirements. The ultimate objective was to complete all levels without losing all lives (by managing stamina).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn, you spend 1 stamina to move in one of four directions (north, south, east, west), unless that direction is blocked by a wall (shown as '#' on the grid). The 'blocked_directions' list tells you which moves are impossible from your current position; attempting a blocked move wastes a turn and stamina. Stepping on a modifier tile cycles either shape (through 6 shapes), color (orange→blue→green→red→orange), or rotation (90° clockwise, wrapping at 270° back to 0°). Goal cells activate only when your piece's current shape, color, and rotation all match the goal's exact requirements simultaneously. You must physically step onto an activated goal to complete it. Stamina refill tiles (yellow rings) restore stamina. Losing all stamina costs a life and resets stamina to max; losing all lives ends the game. The game progresses through multiple levels, each with its own set of goals and modifier placements.
